name: Larry Goldings
abstract: Lawrence Sam Goldings (born August 28, 1968) is a Grammy-nominated American jazz pianist, organist, and composer. His music has explored elements of funk, blues, and fusion.

interviews.title: Larry Gold interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 18, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I went out to Larry and played some with him and it was great. Then Larry came to New York, and we came together, and went into the studio, recorded some stuff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: There was one I had as a student at the New School and he’s recording on Verve now. He’s also playing organ. That’s Larry Goldings.
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Marini
about_quotes.evidence: playing with James Taylor’s band night to night, Larry Goldings, Michael Landau, Steve Gadd, Jimmy Johnson, Walt Fowler
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Marini interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Monheit
about_quotes.evidence: with the exception of Larry Goldings who plays a tune on the album and is a friend of ours, a friend of the band, but we just hadn’t played together before.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Monheit interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 20, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: guys like Larry Goldings and Freddy Bryant, a young guitarist I’ve been working with for quite a while
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 25, 2002
